title: Downtown White Plains, NY 2010-05-20
altText: Street-level view of Downtown White Plains with storefronts, mid-rise buildings, and active sidewalks.
note: Street-level downtown image that fits the neighborhood's walkable commercial core.

title: Dr. Martin Luther King Drive - White Plains, NY and the White Plains Library
altText: View along Dr. Martin Luther King Drive in White Plains with the public library in the foreground and civic buildings beyond.
note: Central White Plains streetscape with the library and courthouse context just off Downtown.

title: Westchester County Government (52640726647)
altText: Exterior view of the Westchester County Government complex in White Plains.
note: County-government building reinforces the civic identity of this central district.

title: White Plains Hospital Winslow Hall Maple Av jeh
altText: White Plains Hospital buildings seen across Maple Avenue.
note: The hospital campus is the clearest rights-clear image tied to the neighborhood's identity.

title: Winbrook Houses, White Plains, New York. LOC gsc.5a21513
altText: Historic view of the Winbrook Houses in White Plains.
attributionText: Gottscho-Schleisner Collection via Library of Congress and Wikimedia Commons
note: Direct historic image of Winbrook's best-known housing complex.

title: White Plains Station Renewal Project (51648065990)
altText: Metro-North's White Plains station after the station renewal project.
photographer: Metropolitan Transportation Authority of the State of New York
attributionText: Metropolitan Transportation Authority via Wikimedia Commons
note: Transit hub image fits the neighborhood's role as an entry point between Downtown and nearby residential areas.

title: WhitePlainsBattleHill01
altText: Battle of White Plains site marker in Battle of White Plains Park.
note: Direct image of the historic marker most closely associated with Battle Hill.

title: Fisher Hill local photo set
altText: Fisher Hill residential street with stone walls, historic homes, and downtown White Plains towers in the distance.
note: User-provided Fisher Hill photo used across Fisher Hill cards and the detail-page hero.

title: View from Chatterton Hill, White Plains (1902)
altText: Panoramic 1902 view from Chatterton Hill, the ridge that later became the Highlands neighborhood, looking east across the rooftops of White Plains.
attributionText: John Rösch, "Picturesque White Plains" (1902), via Internet Archive
note: Historic 1902 photo captioned "View from Chatterton Hill." from Picturesque White Plains. Chatterton Hill — the ridge that anchored the 1776 Battle of White Plains — is the spine of today's Highlands neighborhood.

title: Bridge between Hartsdale and White Plains
altText: Historic bridge view along the Bronx River Parkway corridor near White Plains.
attributionText: Gilmore D. Clarke via Wikimedia Commons
note: Public-domain corridor image fits the neighborhood's river-edge identity better than a generic city view.

altText: The chapel at the Good Counsel Complex in White Plains.

title: St. Mary's Lake and the New Reservoir, White Plains (1902)
altText: A tranquil 1902 view of St. Mary's Lake (the White Plains municipal reservoir), woods reflecting off the still water.
attributionText: John Rösch, "Picturesque White Plains" (1902), via Internet Archive
note: Historic 1902 photo from a full-page plate titled "St. Mary's Lake and the New Reservoir" in Picturesque White Plains — the municipal reservoir that gives this neighborhood its name.

title: Bloomingdale Hospital, White Plains (1902)
altText: Bloomingdale Hospital (the Kirkbride-plan psychiatric campus) in White Plains, photographed around 1902, showing the half-timbered reception building with its clock tower.
attributionText: John Rösch, "Picturesque White Plains" (1902), via Internet Archive
note: Historic 1902 photo captioned "Bloomingdale." from Picturesque White Plains — Bloomingdale Hospital, the Kirkbride-plan psychiatric campus that opened on Bloomingdale Road in 1894 and gives this neighborhood its name.

title: Gedney Farm, residence of Howard Willets, White Plains (1902)
altText: The Gedney Farm residence of Howard Willets, a large shingle-style country house on a rise above Mamaroneck Avenue, photographed in 1902.
attributionText: John Rösch, "Picturesque White Plains" (1902), via Internet Archive
note: Historic 1902 photo captioned "Gedney Farm. Residence of Howard Willets." from Picturesque White Plains — the Gedney family estate the neighborhood is named after, pre-dating the later Gedney Farm Hotel.

title: Rocky Dell, residence of N.C. Reynal, White Plains (1902)
altText: Rocky Dell, the 1902 residence of N.C. Reynal, a Queen Anne-style house on the Rocky Dell estate in White Plains.
attributionText: John Rösch, "Picturesque White Plains" (1902), via Internet Archive
note: Historic 1902 photo captioned "Rocky Dell. Residence of N.C. Reynal." from Picturesque White Plains — the house the neighborhood takes its name from.
